seoTitle: Downer vs Dunlop — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Downer and Dunlop. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Downer and Dunlop.
heading: Downer vs Dunlop — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Downer, Australian Capital Territory and Dunlop, Australian Capital Territory. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Downer and Dunlop have similar overall climate risk scores.

faq.question: Is Downer or Dunlop safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Downer has a flood risk score of 54/100, while Dunlop scores 53/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Downer scores 21/100 for bushfire risk. Dunlop scores 19/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Downer or Dunlop?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Downer has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.35% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
